violations.text: Recently cut tomatoes were observed held in the prep unit at 54 degrees Fahrenheit; recently sliced roast was observed held at 49-50 degrees in the reach-in. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Tomatoes were unwrapped to facilitate air flow. Bags of sliced roast were placed in walk-n to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2025

Working containers of sauces/dressings are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

The following were observed in poor repair: -a probe or sensor at the ventilation hood observed wrapped with paper towel and plastic (inspector discussed with PIC; PIC to inform owner) -handles to walk-in refrigerator and freezer (held with screwdrivers -walk-in freezer observed with an accumulation of ice -cloth observed wrapped around drain line to front service ice bin. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (tomato slicer, and pie wedger,) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Items were taken to be cleaned and sanitized before use. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Bagged ice will be used until ice machine has been cleaned & sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings observed in cupboard and on shelf below check-out area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the front counter area, kitchen, and dry storage room. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the table beneath the grill, toaster, counter island, and beef cooker cart have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. Cutting board storage shelf, shelf above prep area, and oven have peeling delivery plastic remnants. The salad prep unit has an accumulation of water & debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024
